preFix.title: Sweet potato pie
preFix.subtitle: Southern American custard pie with roasted sweet potato
preFix.excerpt: Sweet potato pie is the American South's answer to pumpkin pie: a spiced custard made from roasted sweet potato in a shortcrust shell. The flavour is deeper and less starchy than pumpkin, with a caramel edge from the natural sugars in the potato. It is lighter in colour and richer tasting. Made the day before, it improves considerably.
preFix.sourceNotes: Sweet potato pie is documented in American Southern cooking from the colonial period, predating the widespread availability of pumpkin puree. It is more deeply rooted in African American culinary tradition than pumpkin pie, having been a staple of Southern Black cooking since the era of enslavement when sweet potatoes were a common crop. Jessica B. Harris documents this history in her scholarship on African American foodways. The pie remains associated with Southern American holiday cooking, particularly Thanksgiving and Christmas.

content.text: Roasting the sweet potato rather than boiling it concentrates its flavour and removes excess water. Pierce the skin, roast whole at 200 degrees C until completely soft, about 45 to 60 minutes depending on size, then scoop out the flesh while still hot and mash or blend until completely smooth. Let it cool before using: adding hot puree to eggs will scramble them.

Roast the sweet potatoes at 200 degrees C fan until completely soft. Scoop out the flesh and mash until smooth; you need about 350 g. Cool completely. Make a shortcrust pastry by rubbing 100 g cold diced butter into 175 g plain flour and 25 g icing sugar, then binding with 1 egg yolk and cold water. Roll out, line a 23 cm pie dish, and chill for 20 minutes. Blind-bake at 200 degrees C fan for 12 minutes, remove the beans and paper, and bake for a further 5 minutes. Reduce the oven to 170 degrees C fan.

Beat 50 g softened butter and 150 g light brown sugar together until combined. Add the cooled sweet potato puree and beat until smooth. Add 2 large eggs, 100 ml whole milk, 1 tsp cinnamon, half a teaspoon of nutmeg, and 1 tsp vanilla extract. Beat until fully combined. Pour into the pastry shell. Bake at 170 degrees C fan for 40 to 45 minutes until the edges are set and the centre wobbles only very slightly. Cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours.

items.problem: The filling is too wet and does not set
items.solution: The sweet potato puree had too much moisture. Roasting rather than boiling avoids this, but some varieties are waterier than others. Spread the mashed potato on a baking tray and put in the oven at 150 degrees C for 10 minutes to dry it out before using.
The sweet potato was not mashed or blended finely enough. Pass it through a sieve, or blend in a food processor before mixing with the other filling ingredients.
